title: eryngium bourgatii
intro: Eryngium bourgatii, also known as Mouse Ears or Alpine Sea Holly, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, thanks to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Eryngium bourgatii include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
